_comment: Damodaran FCFF inputs for Astera Labs (ALAB). Amounts in USD millions.
_data_source: Q1 2026 10-Q (SEC EDGAR), FY2025 and FY2024 earnings releases. TTM = Q2+Q3+Q4 2025 + Q1 2026.
_note: ALAB IPO March 2024. Limited financial history. R&D history (pre-2024) estimated from public signals. DCF requires conservative assumptions given early stage and extreme valuation.
_rd_note: TTM R&D (Q2 2025 est $80M + Q3 2025 est $95M + Q4 2025 est $110M + Q1 2026 actual $125.6M = $410.6M). Q1 2025 actual was $64.6M; Q1 2026 actual from 10-Q. Pre-Q1 2025 quarters estimated by interpolation from known data points.
_rd_history_note: Oldest first: FY2022 ~$50M est (private, early stage), FY2023 ~$70M est (private, pre-IPO scale-up), FY2024 ~$180M est (Q1 2025 was $64.6M; Q4 2024 est ~$60M implies ~$180M FY2024), FY2025 ~$350M est (extrapolated from quarterly growth from $64.6M in Q1 to ~$110M in Q4).
story_narrative: Astera Labs makes the connectivity chips — PCIe Gen 5/6 retimers, CXL retimers, Scorpio X-Series AI fabric switches, and now NVLink Fusion + UALink custom silicon — that physically enable AI clusters to operate at scale regardless of GPU architecture. Q1 2026: $308.4M revenue (+93% YoY), 76.3% GAAP gross margin, Q2 guided $355–365M (+18% QoQ). PCIe Gen 6 is >1/3 of Q1 revenue. Scorpio X 320-lane confirmed shipping to hyperscalers; first live public demo at Computex June 3, 2026. Computex June 3 disclosures (material thesis upgrades): (1) Intelligent Connectivity Platform now formally integrates UALink™ alongside NVLink Fusion — ALAB is officially stack-agnostic, serving both NVIDIA (NVLink Fusion) and AMD/non-NVIDIA (UALink) ecosystems; (2) second custom CXL KV Cache design win with a new (unnamed) hyperscaler — first CXL custom silicon win beyond Amazon, shipping for revenue in 2027; (3) NVLink Fusion custom silicon design win with NVIDIA + a hyperscaler for hybrid racks. Amazon Trainium3 Scorpio X design win remains the primary near-term revenue lever. CXL memory pooling (2027+) is now advancing from pilot to custom-chip commitment across at least two hyperscalers. Balance sheet: $1.2B cash, zero financial debt. Risk: at $324.19 (~$59B market cap), stock trades at >100x forward non-GAAP EPS; even at 35% CAGR the DCF yields IV $111.84 — MoS -65.5% SELL/AVOID. The thesis is confirmed and strengthened; the price remains the problem.
story_revenue_cagr: 35% CAGR over 10 years from TTM base of $1,001.5M. This implies reaching ~$19.8B by year 10. Aggressive but directionally supported: FY2026 run rate already tracking ~$1.4–1.6B (annualizing Q2 guide), representing 60–90% YoY growth. Near-term momentum exceeds 35%; the CAGR assumption becomes more demanding as the base grows. Bull case: CXL memory pooling adds an entirely new market layer on top of PCIe retimers and switches, extending 30%+ growth into 2028–2030. Bear case: retimer commoditization and NVIDIA architecture changes slow growth materially by 2028.
story_target_margin: 28% EBIT margin target at end of high-growth period. Current GAAP margin is 20% (R&D-capitalization-adjusted starting margin is ~40% — the model transitions down as R&D investment normalizes relative to revenue). Fabless model and high gross margins (76.3%) support long-run operating leverage. 28% is attainable as SBC and R&D stabilize relative to revenue — consistent with mature fabless semis like Lattice Semiconductor.
story_growth_years: 10 years of high growth: Phase 1 (2026–2028) PCIe Gen 6 retimer + Scorpio X switch ramp into Amazon Trainium3, Google TPU, Microsoft Azure AI clusters; Phase 2 (2028–2030) CXL memory pooling adoption as AI model sizes exceed single-GPU HBM; Phase 3 (2030–2032) next connectivity standard. The 10-year horizon is justified by the secular AI infrastructure buildout and the multi-generational protocol upgrade cycle.
story_stable_growth: 3% long-run growth matching nominal GDP. Connectivity chips become standardized infrastructure — growth slows to volumes tracking new server shipments. CXL by then is mature and commoditizing.
story_stable_margin: 22% stable EBIT — connectivity chips commoditize over time as the PCIe/CXL standard matures. Margins compress from the 28% peak as competition intensifies (Broadcom, Marvell, next entrants). Still above the semiconductor industry average given fabless model and IP moat in signal integrity.
story_s2c_high: 2.5x sales-to-capital — fabless model, very light CapEx (~$8M TTM). Primary investment is R&D headcount and TSMC NRE costs for new chip designs. Revenue growth requires proportionally less capital than asset-heavy manufacturers.
story_s2c_stable: 2.8x — steady-state improvement as chip IP is reused across customers and product generations. Software-defined connectivity features (CXL memory pooling management) may add recurring software-like revenue with higher S/C ratios.
story_beta: 1.90 high-growth beta — very high systematic risk: revenue concentrated in AI server BOM (single-market exposure), CXL timing is uncertain (standard still maturing), limited 2-year financial history, and stock has exhibited extreme volatility (+69% in 25 days). Beta will compress toward 1.20 stable as revenue diversifies and the business matures.
story_rd_cap: 5-year amortization. PCIe/CXL protocol IP has useful life tied to the spec generation (PCIe 5→6→7, each ~3–5 years). R&D capitalization is material: TTM R&D ~$411M vs TTM GAAP EBIT ~$200M. Capitalizing R&D reveals the true earnings power of the business — the reported GAAP margin understates economic earnings significantly. Atreides Management's conviction ($369M position) partly reflects undervalued R&D IP not visible in GAAP EPS.
story_uncertainty: High — single-market revenue concentration (AI server BOM), CXL timing is uncertain, very limited public financial history (IPO March 2024), extreme valuation multiple (~112x forward EPS), and recent +69% stock appreciation in 25 days. Requires widest MoS of all 7 picks: need >30% MoS discount for a BUY signal. At $309.78, DCF validation is critical.
